 {"id":"bd-385a","title":"Interactive: represent tool calls/results as first-class chat items","description":"# Goal
Refactor interactive conversation rendering so tool calls and tool results are modeled as distinct message/item types.

# Background
Legacy UI can collapse/expand tool output because tool calls/results are separate components.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Extend the in-memory conversation representation to include:
  - user message
  - assistant message
  - assistant thinking block(s)
  - tool call start (name + args)
  - tool result (stdout/stderr/exit + metadata)
  - system notices
- Ensure items can be rendered with consistent styling.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tool calls/results are no longer “just text”; they are structured items.
- [ ] Existing agent event integration feeds these items correctly.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-389q","title":"Task: Create ExtensionDispatcher struct and HostcallHandler trait","description":"# Task: Create ExtensionDispatcher Struct

## Objective

Create the foundational ExtensionDispatcher struct that will coordinate all hostcall routing. This is the first step in the hostcall dispatcher feature.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
Create new file: `src/extension_dispatcher.rs`

### Core Struct

```rust
use std::sync::Arc;
use crate::extensions_js::{PiJsRuntime, HostcallRequest, HostcallOutcome, HostcallKind};
use crate::tools::ToolRegistry;
use crate::extensions::{ExtensionSession, ExtensionUiHandler};
use crate::connectors::http::HttpConnector;

/// Coordinates hostcall dispatch between JS extension runtime and Rust implementations.
/// 
/// The dispatcher is the critical integration layer that makes extensions functional.
/// It processes pending hostcall requests from the JS runtime and routes them to
/// appropriate handlers (tools, HTTP, session, UI, etc.).
pub struct ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// The JavaScript runtime that generates hostcall requests
    runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
    
    /// Registry of available tools (built-in + extension-registered)
    tool_registry: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    
    /// HTTP connector for pi.http() calls
    http_connector: Arc<HttpConnector>,
    
    /// Session access for pi.session() calls
    session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession + Send + Sync>,
    
    /// UI handler for pi.ui() calls (TUI or RPC implementation)
    ui_handler: Arc<dyn ExtensionUiHandler + Send + Sync>,
    
    /// Current working directory for relative path resolution
    cwd: PathBuf,
}

/// Trait for handling individual hostcall types.
/// Each hostcall kind has a dedicated handler implementation.
#[async_trait]
pub trait HostcallHandler: Send + Sync {
    /// Process a hostcall request and return the outcome.
    async fn handle(&self, params: serde_json::Value) -> HostcallOutcome;
    
    /// The capability name for policy checking (e.g., "read", "exec", "http")
    fn capability(&self) -> &'static str;
}
```

### Constructor

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    pub fn new(
        runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
        tool_registry: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
        http_connector: Arc<HttpConnector>,
        session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession + Send + Sync>,
        ui_handler: Arc<dyn ExtensionUiHandler + Send + Sync>,
        cwd: PathBuf,
    ) -> Self {
        Self {
            runtime,
            tool_registry,
            http_connector,
            session,
            ui_handler,
            cwd,
        }
    }
}
```

### Module Export

Add to `src/lib.rs`:
```rust
pub mod extension_dispatcher;
pub use extension_dispatcher::ExtensionDispatcher;
```

## Testing Strategy

1. Unit test: Dispatcher construction with mock dependencies
2. Unit test: Empty hostcall queue returns immediately
3. Integration test: Verify drain_hostcall_requests() is called

## Dependencies

- Depends on: None (first task)
- Blocks: All other hostcall handler tasks

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ExtensionDispatcher struct defined with all required fields
- [ ] HostcallHandler trait defined
- [ ] Constructor implemented
- [ ] Module exported from lib.rs
- [ ] Compiles without errors
- [ ] Basic unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:18.042960108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:20:10.607391633Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:20:10.607327593Z","close_reason":"ExtensionDispatcher + HostcallHandler landed (src/extension_dispatcher.rs + exports); gates green; pushed dd02371","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-389q","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:18.052447970Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38gy","title":"Interactive: hideThinkingBlock setting + toggleThinking action","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy thinking visibility controls.

# Required Behavior
- Setting `hide_thinking_block` (legacy: hideThinkingBlock):
  - when true, thinking blocks are hidden by default.
- Action `toggleThinking` (legacy default Ctrl+T):
  - toggles thinking visibility at runtime.

# Dependencies
- Keybinding router (`bd-gze`) for Ctrl+T.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Thinking can be hidden via settings.
- [ ] Ctrl+T toggles thinking visibility.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-3984","title":"Conformance: nicobailon extensions (6 exts, npm packages)","description":"Conformance tests for nicobailon's popular npm-published Pi extensions. Extensions (6): 1. pi-mcp-adapter (npm) — Token-efficient MCP server integration, proxy tool, lazy loading 2. pi-subagents (npm) — Async subagent delegation with truncation and artifacts 3. pi-interactive-shell (npm v0.7.1) — Full PTY emulation overlay, no tmux, user takeover 4. pi-interview-tool — Web-based form tool with keyboard navigation 5. pi-powerline-footer — Powerline-style status bar with git integration 6. pi-rewind-hook — Rewind file changes with git-based checkpoints.  {"id":"bd-39jj","title":"Conformance: Tier 1a — Tool-Only Extensions (7 exts)","description":"Conformance tests for the simplest extension category: single-file extensions that only register tools (no events, no commands, no UI). These test the core pi.registerTool() pathway.

Extensions (7):
1. hello.ts — Minimal greeting tool, returns text + details payload
2. tools.ts — Registers multiple tools demonstrating the full tool API
3. tool-override.ts — Overrides built-in tool behavior (wraps for logging/ACL)
4. truncated-tool.ts — Wraps ripgrep with output truncation
5. question.ts — Interactive question tool
6. questionnaire.ts — Multi-question questionnaire tool
7. qna.ts — Q&A extraction tool

For each extension:
- Load in QuickJS, verify registration succeeds
- Invoke each registered tool with sample parameters
- Assert output content matches reference (text, details, is_error)
- Assert tool schema (parameters JSON Schema) matches reference
- Test error cases (missing params, invalid input)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:15:45.253645023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:27:42.152775194Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:27:42.152577084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39jj","depends_on_id":"bd-s2zr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:15:45.297232749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 ## Stratified Extension Sampling Matrix
 
-This matrix uses **deterministic sampling criteria** from `docs/EXTENSION_CANDIDATES.md` (popularity rubric + compatibility status), plus the raw candidate pool and coverage tags, to select a target sample size of **16** (min **12**, max **20**). It defines **axes + quotas** and maps **every candidate** to the axes so selection can be mechanical and reproducible.
+This matrix uses **deterministic sampling criteria** from `docs/EXTENSION_CANDIDATES.md` (popularity rubric + compatibility status), plus the raw candidate pool (`docs/extension-candidate-pool.json`) and coverage tags, to select a target sample size of **16** (min **12**, max **20**). It defines **axes + quotas** and maps **every candidate** to the axes so selection can be mechanical and reproducible.
 
 > **Note:** Tags below are **inferred** from README/descriptions. A static scan should validate and adjust before final selection.
